title: Rich Gooey Argentinian Cheese Empanadas
description: Hearty, single‑serve empanadas that melt with a burst of harissa‑spiced cheese, perfect for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: mixed cheese (queso de mano & mozzarella), shredded

instructions: In a bowl combine flour, salt and 1 tbsp olive oil; gradually whisk in water until a soft dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 3 minutes, then cover with a damp cloth and let rest 30 minutes.
While the dough rests, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium heat; add onion and garlic, sauté until translucent.
Stir in harissa paste and smoked paprika; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the shredded cheese mix to the skillet, stir until the cheese starts to soften and the mixture is gooey; remove from heat and splash with lemon juice and parsley.
Divide the rested dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a 6-inch circle on a lightly floured surface.
Place a heaping spoonful of the cheese filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half‑moon shape and pinch edges together; seal with a fork.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place the empanadas on the sheet, leaving space between them, and bake for 18–20 minutes until golden brown.
Let the empanadas cool for 5 minutes before serving. Enjoy while they're warm and gooey.
